Title: A novel transcription factor CmMYB012 inhibits flavone and anthocyanin biosynthesis in response to high temperatures in chrysanthemum

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native CmFNS promoter (990 bp) in Y1H/LUC reporters; no heterologous promoter for stable expression reported
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Flavone biosynthesis within the flavonoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: CmFNS is a direct engineering target to maintain or increase flavone accumulation under prolonged heat, improving ROS scavenging and heat tolerance in vegetative tissues. Best deployment would be heat-stable or tissue-specific activation in leaves, potentially stacked with anthocyanin-promoting regulators for ornamental quality, while avoiding excessive constitutive diversion that could perturb other flavonoid branches.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts flavanones to flavones in the flavonoid pathway, supporting apigenin/luteolin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher CmFNS expression is associated with increased flavone accumulation and improved tolerance to prolonged high temperature; repression of CmFNS lowers flavones and weakens heat resilience.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR under heat and in CmMYB012 OE/RNAi lines; promoter binding by Y1H, ChIP-PCR and EMSA through upstream regulator CmMYB012; promoter repression by dual-luciferase assay; HPLC metabolite profiling of flavones.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Prolonged high temperature (35°C for 6 days; also early time-course under heat)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in chrysanthemum; transcript markedly decreased after prolonged high temperature treatment; relatively maintained at higher levels in RNAi-CmMYB012 plants under heat.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CmFNS was transiently upregulated during the first 6 h of heat treatment, then downregulated after prolonged heat, implying additional unidentified positive regulators and partial CmMYB012 dependence.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced expression lowers flavones, increases ROS damage, cell death and heat sensitivity; excessive enhancement could alter carbon flux among competing flavonoid branches, though this was not directly tested.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: 2-oxoglutarate-dependent oxygenase flavonoid biosynthetic enzyme (paper functionally treats as FNS)
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-control step for flavone accumulation under heat; rate-limiting not formally established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CmFNS expression declines after prolonged high temperature, paralleling reduced leaf flavone content. 2. CmMYB012 directly binds the CmFNS promoter AACATT element and represses its transcription. 3. Higher CmFNS activity supports flavone accumulation for ROS scavenging and heat tolerance in leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves; also flowers were assayed for flavone content in some contexts
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ROS scavenging and protection against high-temperature stress

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native promoters used in Y1H/LUC reporters; no stable overexpression construct reported for these pathway genes
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Anthocyanin biosynthesis within the flavonoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: CmCHS is a useful upstream engineering target for sustaining anthocyanin formation and pink flower color under heat, especially if edited to reduce CmMYB012-mediated repression or combined with positive anthocyanin regulators. Because CHS sits upstream of multiple flavonoid branches, manipulation may have broader metabolic effects.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the condensation of p-coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to form chalcone, the entry step to flavonoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Repression of CmCHS by CmMYB012 contributes to lower anthocyanin accumulation and weaker pink flower coloration, especially under heat.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter interaction by Y1H; promoter repression by dual-luciferase assay; RT-qPCR in CmMYB012 OE/RNAi plants under normal and high temperature; anthocyanin phenotyping.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal and high temperature, particularly prolonged/high-temperature flowering conditions
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript suppressed in CmMYB012-OE plants and elevated in RNAi-CmMYB012 plants; heat suppression partially restored by CmMYB012 silencing.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contains AACATT element(s) in promoter recognized by CmMYB012. Affects both anthocyanin and potentially other flavonoid branch flux due to upstream position.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: General entry step feeding anthocyanin/flavone/flavonol branches
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Because CHS is upstream, enhancing it may broadly alter flavonoid balance beyond anthocyanins.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Committed entry step; specific rate-limiting status in this paper not established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CmMYB012 directly binds the CmCHS promoter. 2. CmMYB012 represses CmCHS promoter activity and transcript accumulation. 3. This repression contributes to reduced anthocyanin biosynthesis and weaker pink flower coloration under heat.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native promoters used in Y1H/LUC reporters; no stable overexpression construct reported for this pathway gene
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Anthocyanin biosynthesis within the flavonoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: CmDFR is a useful downstream target to preserve anthocyanin accumulation and ornamental color under heat. Editing the promoter to weaken CmMYB012 binding or stacking with activators may be effective with fewer upstream pleiotropic effects than CHS manipulation.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces dihydroflavonols to leucoanthocyanidins in the anthocyanin/proanthocyanidin pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Repression of CmDFR by CmMYB012 lowers anthocyanin biosynthesis and pink flower intensity, especially under high temperature.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter interaction by Y1H; promoter repression by dual-luciferase assay; RT-qPCR in transgenic chrysanthemum lines under normal/high temperature; anthocyanin measurement.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High temperature during flowering; also normal temperature controls
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript level tracks anthocyanin phenotype; reduced in CmMYB012-OE and relatively restored in RNAi-CmMYB012 plants, with stronger repression under heat.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter contains AACATT element(s) recognized by CmMYB012.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower expression reduces flower pigmentation; enhanced expression may redirect flux from competing flavonoid branches.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Important downstream control point for anthocyanin accumulation; rate-limiting not formally established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CmMYB012 directly targets the CmDFR promoter. 2. CmMYB012 represses CmDFR expression and promoter activity. 3. Reduced CmDFR expression contributes to lower anthocyanin accumulation under high temperature.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native promoters used in Y1H/LUC reporters; no stable overexpression construct reported for this pathway gene
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Anthocyanin biosynthesis within the flavonoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: CmANS is a favorable downstream target for maintaining anthocyanin output and pink flower color under heat. Promoter editing to remove CmMYB012-responsive motif(s) could specifically enhance pigmentation with potentially narrower metabolic side effects than upstream genes.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes leucoanthocyanidins to anthocyanidins in anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CmMYB012-mediated repression of CmANS decreases anthocyanin production and pink flower color, especially under high temperature.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter interaction by Y1H; promoter repression by dual-luciferase assay; RT-qPCR in OE/RNAi lines; anthocyanin phenotyping.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High temperature during flowering; normal temperature comparison
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reduced in CmMYB012-OE plants and elevated/partially restored in RNAi-CmMYB012 plants; heat suppresses expression.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter contains AACATT element(s) recognized by CmMYB012.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced expression diminishes flower market value through paler color; stronger expression could alter flux into colored flavonoids.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Important late biosynthetic step; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CmMYB012 binds the CmANS promoter. 2. CmMYB012 represses CmANS transcriptional activity and expression. 3. This repression contributes to heat-associated anthocyanin loss and weaker pink flower color.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native promoters used in Y1H/LUC reporters; no stable overexpression construct reported for this pathway gene
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Anthocyanin biosynthesis within the flavonoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: CmUFGT is a practical target for stabilizing final anthocyanin accumulation and flower color under heat. Promoter editing to reduce CmMYB012 repression may improve pigment stability while minimizing wider pathway perturbation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Repression of CmUFGT by CmMYB012 decreases stable anthocyanin accumulation and pink flower coloration, particularly at high temperature.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter interaction by Y1H; promoter repression by dual-luciferase assay; RT-qPCR in CmMYB012 OE/RNAi lines under normal and high temperature; anthocyanin phenotyping.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High temperature during flowering; normal temperature comparison
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Suppressed in CmMYB012-OE plants and promoted in RNAi-CmMYB012 plants; high-temperature suppression partially restored by CmMYB012 silencing.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter contains AACATT element(s) recognized by CmMYB012.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced activity lowers stable anthocyanin deposition and ornamental value; enhanced activity could alter glycosylated flavonoid profiles.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant of stable anthocyanin accumulation; not formally designated rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CmMYB012 directly binds the CmUFGT promoter. 2. CmMYB012 represses CmUFGT promoter activity and transcript accumulation. 3. The repression lowers stable anthocyanin accumulation and pink flower coloration under high temperature.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression (35S:CmMYB012-GFP; pORE-R4-CmMYB012); RNAi construct for suppression; target native promoters assayed include CmFNS, CmCHS, CmDFR, CmANS, and CmUFGT
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: CmMYB012 is a strong target for genome editing, silencing, or promoter weakening to improve heat tolerance and preserve pink flower color under warming conditions. Optimal deployment would likely use heat-responsive attenuation or tissue-specific suppression to maintain vegetative flavones and floral anthocyanins while minimizing unintended developmental effects. It could be combined with positive flavonoid regulators or promoter edits in direct targets such as CmFNS/CmDFR/CmUFGT.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CmMYB012 is induced by prolonged high temperature and represses both flavone and anthocyanin biosynthesis, causing lower flavone/anthocyanin accumulation, reduced heat tolerance, increased ROS damage, and paler pink flowers.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H screening and validation; ChIP-PCR; EMSA; dual-luciferase promoter repression assays; stable overexpression and RNAi in chrysanthemum; RT-qPCR of targets; HPLC/metabolite assays; anthocyanin quantification; heat-stress phenotyping.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent master negative regulator of flavone decline and anthocyanin suppression under prolonged high temperature; decisive because its manipulation shifts both metabolite levels and associated heat-tolerance/flower-color phenotypes.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Prolonged high temperature (35°C in leaves; 35/24°C cycles during flowering); normal temperature controls; apigenin rescue experiments support downstream flavone function
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: No epigenetic regulation reported; protein lacks EAR motif, suggesting passive repression rather than corepressor-mediated chromatin repression.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in flower organs; relatively low in vegetative organs; markedly induced by prolonged high temperature, but not strongly changed during the first 3 h of heat treatment.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Not directly demonstrated; discussion proposes competition with an unknown positive regulator for CmFNS promoter occupancy under prolonged heat.
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CmFNS promoter; CmCHS promoter; CmDFR promoter; CmANS promoter; CmUFGT promoter
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional control of flavone and anthocyanin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A novel atypical SG7 R2R3-MYB lacking conserved SG7/SG7-2 motifs and lacking transcriptional activation activity. It specifically binds AACATT element(s). It did not regulate CmCHI, CmF3H, or CmFLS and did not affect flavonol accumulation. Authors infer it likely acts as a passive repressor competing with activators. Ectopic expression in tobacco also reduced floral anthocyanins, suggesting conserved negative function.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression lowers plant fitness under heat, increases ROS accumulation and cell death, and reduces ornamental/commercial value through paler flowers. Suppression improves heat tolerance and anthocyanin retention; no clear effect on flower size reduction under heat, which is CmMYB012-independent.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: No experimentally validated complex reported; discussion suggests it may act alone or potentially with a bHLH factor, but this was not resolved.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Atypical SG7 R2R3-MYB transcription factor lacking canonical SG7/SG7-2 motifs; no EAR repression motif detected
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional repression/inactivation of target promoters via AACATT motif binding, likely passive repression through competition with activators
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses/inactivates CmFNS, CmCHS, CmDFR, CmANS, and CmUFGT transcription
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CmMYB012 is a heat-induced atypical SG7 R2R3-MYB that binds AACATT cis-elements. 2. It directly represses CmFNS to reduce flavone accumulation, weakening ROS scavenging and heat tolerance. 3. It also directly represses CmCHS, CmDFR, CmANS, and CmUFGT to inhibit anthocyanin biosynthesis and pink flower coloration under heat.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves for flavones; petals/flowers for anthocyanins
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavones: ROS scavenging and heat-stress protection; Anthocyanins: flower coloration and antioxidant protection
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Prolonged high temperature; possibly ROS-related heat signaling; unknown upstream regulators are proposed but not identified
